spec_match.rationale: Chrome DevTools MCP verified the actual input, contenteditable, and URL-field surfaces all remain computed as text-overflow:ellipsis while focused with overflowing content, matching the Chrome 148 CSS rendering behaviour.

interactive.rationale: The page now has controls for focusing each distinct surface, switching to a short no-overflow input edge case, restoring long values, and blurring the active field.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The three examples match the feature promise for form controls and contenteditable text without claiming a separate JavaScript API.

edge_cases.rationale: The Short text edge control sets all values to fitting content and the probe reports overflowed=false for the focused input.

references.rationale: A labelled references section links to ChromeStatus feature 5146265241387008, CSS Overflow 3 ellipsis interaction, and the Chrome 148 beta note.

design_system.rationale: The added controls and probe use shared CSS variables, and button background overrides include explicit text colour overrides.

summary: Chrome DevTools MCP on Chrome/151 verified long input, contenteditable, and URL examples as active overflowing ellipsis states, the short input edge as non-overflowing, all reference links present, and no console or network errors. The feature conformance route reported 5 PASS / 0 FAIL.
